description:
You are an MLOps engineer working in the directory  
    /home/user/experiments  

    Two files already exist:

    1. /home/user/experiments/experiment_metrics.csv  
       • Comma-separated, Unix line-endings.  
       • Current contents:  
         experiment_id,accuracy,loss  
         1,0.85,0.45  
         2,0.88,0.40  

    2. /home/user/experiments/last_run_config.json  
       • Single-line JSON document (no pretty-print, no trailing newline).  
       • Current contents:  
         {"lr":0.001,"accuracy":0.93,"loss":0.35}

    Your tasks:

    A. Append the results of the most recent run to experiment_metrics.csv.  
       • Determine the next sequential experiment_id (it must be exactly one greater than the
         largest id already present).  
       • Extract accuracy and loss from last_run_config.json.  
       • Append ONE new line to the CSV in the format:  
         <experiment_id>,<accuracy>,<loss>  
         (No extra spaces before/after commas, Unix newline at the end.)

    B. Create /home/user/experiments/best_accuracy.json that reports the single best
       experiment so far.  
       • JSON must be one compact line, no spaces, no newline.  
       • Exact key order: experiment_id,accuracy  
       • Example format: {"experiment_id":3,"accuracy":0.93}

    C. Produce a plain-text verification log named /home/user/experiments/update_log.txt  
       • Exactly two lines, Unix line ends, no leading/trailing spaces.  
       • Line 1: rows=<total_number_of_data_rows_in_CSV_after_append>  
       • Line 2: best_accuracy=<highest_accuracy_value_present_in_CSV>  
       • Example shape:  
         rows=3  
         best_accuracy=0.93

    Important output rules that the automated grader will enforce:
       • experiment_metrics.csv must end with exactly 3 data rows (+ the header).  
       • best_accuracy.json must be a single compact JSON line, no whitespace.  
       • update_log.txt must contain exactly the two lines described, nothing else.  
       • Do NOT modify or delete any other files.

    Accomplish everything using normal command-line tools you would have in a standard
    Debian/Ubuntu-based container; no GUI applications are available.
truth:
Initial files before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/experiments/experiment_metrics.csv  
       experiment_id,accuracy,loss\n
       1,0.85,0.45\n
       2,0.88,0.40\n

    2. /home/user/experiments/last_run_config.json  
       {"lr":0.001,"accuracy":0.93,"loss":0.35}

    Expected final state after successful completion:

    A. /home/user/experiments/experiment_metrics.csv  
       experiment_id,accuracy,loss\n
       1,0.85,0.45\n
       2,0.88,0.40\n
       3,0.93,0.35\n

       • Note: final newline present at end of file.

    B. /home/user/experiments/best_accuracy.json  
       {"experiment_id":3,"accuracy":0.93}

    C. /home/user/experiments/update_log.txt  
       rows=3\n
       best_accuracy=0.93\n
